name: Ananya Iyer
bio: Ananya spent years reviewing SCADA and monthly generation reports for 100MW+ portfolios in Rajasthan and Gujarat. She writes about soiling curves, performance ratio gaps, and how to prove cleaning ROI with meter data—not headline renewable energy news.

name: Arjun Sharma
bio: Arjun explains lifecycle Opex, cleaning contract structures, and what IPPs should diligence before robotics or AMC renewals. His angle is investor and asset-owner economics on Indian utility-scale plants.

name: Kavya Reddy
bio: Kavya focuses on water use at arid and semi-arid sites, dust behaviour on glass, and when waterless robotic cleaning is the only scalable option for daily cycles. She bridges environmental constraints with field-ready O&M practice.

name: Manpreet Singh
bio: Manpreet writes from an EPC handover perspective: row spacing, tracker tolerances, cable management, and owner specs that prevent costly rework when autonomous cleaners are commissioned. Utility-scale construction in India is his lane.

name: Prathamesh Kulkarni
bio: Prathamesh documents autonomous cleaning robots, docking stations, fleet dashboards, and block-by-block deployment on 50MW+ sites. He cares about what works on fixed-tilt and tracker rows in live Indian conditions.

name: Rohan Mehta
bio: Rohan covers weather-aware scheduling, remote monitoring portals, and where ML actually improves cleaning timing versus marketing buzz. He translates industrial IoT concepts for plant O&M leads.

name: Rohit Jadhav
bio: Rohit publishes practical guidance for plant managers: shift planning, HSE around energised fields, scaling crews across blocks, and when to shift from manual washing to automation. His audience is operations, not residential installers.

name: Saurabh Patil
bio: Saurabh compares manual brush crews, semi-automatic systems, and full autonomous robots with honest trade-offs on labour, water, cycle time, and night-time production windows. He has commissioned cleaning methods on large Madhya Pradesh plants.

name: Suraj Kadam
bio: Suraj sets editorial direction for utility-scale solar, robotic cleaning, and India O&M policy. He turns project interviews and operating data into long-form guides for asset owners, EPC teams, and service contractors.

name: Tejaswini Joshi
bio: Tejaswini decodes AMC scopes, SLA metrics, penalty clauses, and how owners should audit contractor performance against robotic or hybrid cleaning programmes. Her posts help renegotiate maintenance spend with evidence.

name: Vaishnavi Deshmukh
bio: Vaishnavi specialises in tracker geometry, inter-row clearance, slope limits, and robot path planning on single-axis sites. She writes technical fit guides—not generic sustainability essays or residential tips.
